title: Stenostomum bifurcatum
taxonomy: Stenostomum bifurcatum is a species of liverwort, a type of non-vascular land plant. As a liverwort, it belongs to the phylum Marchantiophyta. Within this phylum, Stenostomum bifurcatum is classified as a member of the class Marchantiopsida. The class Marchantiopsida further divides into the order Marchantiales, and Stenostomum bifurcatum is a part of this order. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics.

intro: Stenostomum bifurcatum, also known as "erva-preta" in Portuguese, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties.
